raw_transcript: That 'immediate' bloating—often within thirty to sixty minutes of eating—is very characteristic of SIBO. Normally, your small intestine is relatively sterile, but in SIBO, bacteria from the colon migrate upward and ferment your food before you can absorb it.
final_corrected_note: That 'immediate' bloating—often within thirty to sixty minutes of eating—is very characteristic of SIBO. Normally, your small intestine is relatively sterile, but in SIBO, bacteria from the colon migrate upward and ferment your food before you can absorb it.
